subject_tmpl: Development Interest — {ADDRESS} on {STREET}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, my name is {FIRST} {LAST} and I represent {BIZ}, a mixed-use development firm based in Oak Park. We have been eyeing the vacant parcel at {ADDRESS} on {STREET} for a potential three-story mixed-income project — ground-floor retail with sixteen residential units above. Before we commission a full feasibility study, I wanted to reach out directly to gauge the Village's interest in partnering and to understand whether any TIF or incentive tools might be available. Could we schedule a brief introductory call?
reply_tmpl: Thank you for reaching out, {FIRST}. The Village is actively seeking quality mixed-use development along {STREET} and I'd be glad to connect you with our village manager and economic development team. I'll have my assistant Tina reach out to schedule a call for the week of {DATE}. We look forward to learning more about {BIZ}'s vision.

subject_tmpl: Vacant Storefront Facade Grant — {ADDRESS} {STREET}
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} here, owner of {BIZ} at {ADDRESS} {STREET}. I've heard Bellwood has a facade improvement grant program and I'd like to apply. We're planning to replace the storefront windows, repaint the exterior, and add new signage — estimated cost around {AMOUNT}. Can you send me the application, eligibility requirements, and any deadlines? We're eager to get started before summer foot traffic picks up.
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, thanks for your interest in the Bellwood Facade Grant Program. I'm attaching the current application packet. The next review cycle closes {DATE}, so please submit all materials — contractor quotes, photos, and property tax clearance — by then. Feel free to call our office at {PHONE} if you have questions.

subject_tmpl: Zoning Variance Request — {BIZ}, {ADDRESS} {STREET}
body_tmpl: Good morning, I'm writing on behalf of {BIZ}. We are proposing to open a full-service restaurant at {ADDRESS} {STREET}, but the space is only {NUM} feet from the adjacent residential lot line — short of the {NUM}-foot setback required under the current zoning ordinance. We believe the unique configuration of the building and our planned hours of operation make us a good candidate for a variance. What is the formal process and timeline, and how do we get on the ZBA calendar?

subject_tmpl: Parking Adequacy Question — New Use at {ADDRESS} {STREET}
body_tmpl: Hi, I'm {FIRST} {LAST}, prospective tenant for the commercial space at {ADDRESS} {STREET}. We plan to open a {BIZ} — primarily a sit-down tutoring and test-prep center with up to {NUM} students at peak hours. The current lot has {NUM} striped spaces. I want to confirm before signing the lease whether this meets village parking requirements for our use classification. Can someone from zoning advise?
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, for an educational/tutoring use the village requires one space per three students at maximum enrollment plus one per employee on the largest shift. If your peak is {NUM} students and {NUM} staff, you'd need {NUM} spaces. The current lot may require a shared-parking agreement or a variance. Please call Rosa Marchetti's office at {PHONE} to set up a pre-application meeting.

subject_tmpl: TIF District Eligibility Question for {STREET} Redevelopment
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST}, {BIZ}. We're evaluating a ground-up commercial redevelopment on the {NUM}-{NUM} block of {STREET}. Several colleagues mentioned Bellwood has an active TIF district in that area. Can you confirm whether the parcels fall within the TIF boundary, what increment revenues currently look like, and whether the Village has used TIF funds to support tenant buildout or infrastructure? Any summary documents would be very helpful at this stage.

subject_tmpl: Cook County CDBG Small Business Grant — Bellwood Eligibility Window
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I'm {FIRST} {LAST} from Cook County's Community Development office. I'm writing to flag that Bellwood-area small businesses may qualify for our next CDBG micro-enterprise grant round — up to {AMOUNT} per business — with applications opening {DATE}. The County can provide a brief outreach session for your business community if the Village can host. Could your office confirm interest by {DATE} so we can lock in a presenter? Happy to share a flyer draft as well.
reply_tmpl: Thank you, {FIRST}. Bellwood would welcome a County outreach session for our business corridor. Tina Alvarez in my office will coordinate logistics and can confirm a date by end of week. We'll also push the grant information through our e-newsletter and social channels ahead of the {DATE} opening.

subject_tmpl: Build-Out Permit Timeline — {BIZ} at {ADDRESS} {STREET}
body_tmpl: My contractor tells me our interior build-out permit for {BIZ} at {ADDRESS} {STREET} has been in review for {DAYS} business days. We have a hard lease commencement date of {DATE} and every week of delay costs us rent on a closed space. Could someone give me a current status and an estimated approval date? Permit reference is {CASE}.
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, permit {CASE} is in final plan review — electrical comments were sent to your contractor on {DATE}. Once the revised drawings are resubmitted we anticipate approval within five business days. Please have your contractor upload the corrected sheets to the portal as soon as possible so we can keep you on track for {DATE}.

subject_tmpl: Interest in Relocating {BIZ} to Bellwood — Next Steps?
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I own {BIZ}, a light-assembly and distribution operation currently in Melrose Park. We employ {NUM} people locally and are looking to expand into a larger facility. A broker showed us a {NUM}-square-foot industrial building near {STREET} and {CROSS}. Bellwood's location near the Metra UP-W line is attractive for our workforce. I'd love to understand what the Village offers for relocating businesses and whether there are any incentive programs we should know about.

subject_tmpl: Unpermitted Storefront Work — {ADDRESS} {STREET} (Case {CASE})
body_tmpl: Rosa, during my corridor walk on {DATE} I observed what appears to be unpermitted exterior alterations at {ADDRESS} {STREET} — new awning installation and partial masonry removal at the storefront. No permit placard was posted. I've issued a stop-work notice and logged it as case {CASE}. Please check whether any permit application is pending and advise on next steps for the business owner. The tenant indicated they were unaware a permit was required.
reply_tmpl: Sandra, I checked the system — no permit application on file for that address. I'll reach out to the property owner of record and the tenant today. If they submit within {DAYS} days we can likely resolve this administratively; otherwise it moves to violation. Keep the stop-work notice posted.

subject_tmpl: Why Is the Old {BIZ} Building on {STREET} Still Empty?
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I live on {STREET} near {CROSS} and have watched the building that used to be {BIZ} sit vacant for what must be {DAYS} days now. The windows are boarded, weeds are taking over the parking lot, and it's become a dumping magnet. Does the Village have any plan to bring a new tenant or owner in? I'd love to see something family-friendly there — a café, a credit union, anything. Just wanted to make sure someone in government was tracking this.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for your message, {FIRST}. The property at {ADDRESS} {STREET} is on our economic development radar. We've been in contact with the owner regarding code compliance and are actively marketing the site to prospective tenants through our corridor program. I'll make sure code enforcement does a pass on the lot condition this week. I appreciate your patience and your advocacy for the neighborhood.

subject_tmpl: Sign Permit Application — {BIZ}, {ADDRESS} {STREET}
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} from {BIZ}. We'd like to install a new illuminated channel-letter sign on the facade of our location at {ADDRESS} {STREET}. The proposed sign is {NUM} square feet, mounted flush to the parapet. I've attached a drawing from our sign company. Can you confirm this is within the allowable sign area for our zoning district and what the permit fee would be? We'd like to have it up before {DATE}.

subject_tmpl: Corridor Vacancy Update — {STREET} Q{NUM} {DATE}
body_tmpl: Mayor, attached is the quarterly vacancy scan for the {STREET} commercial corridor. We currently show {NUM} vacant storefronts between {CROSS} and {CROSS}. Of those, {NUM} have active permit applications, {NUM} are in lease negotiation per our broker contacts, and {NUM} remain stalled — primarily due to ownership disputes or deferred maintenance. I recommend we schedule a targeted outreach meeting with those property owners in the next {DAYS} days. Let me know if you'd like to discuss before I send invitations.

subject_tmpl: Business License Renewal — {BIZ}, License #{ACCT}
body_tmpl: Hi, I'm {FIRST} {LAST}, owner of {BIZ} at {ADDRESS} {STREET}. I received a renewal notice for license #{ACCT} expiring {DATE}. I've updated our hours and added a second location manager since last year. Do I need to file any supplemental paperwork for those changes, or does the standard renewal form cover it? Also, is online payment available this year?
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, the standard renewal form does cover updated hours, but adding a manager requires a supplemental personnel disclosure. I'm attaching the form — just complete the new manager's section and return it with your renewal. Online payment via the Village portal is available this year; use your license number {ACCT} to log in. Renewal deadline is {DATE}.

subject_tmpl: FOIA Request — TIF District Annual Reports, {STREET}
body_tmpl: Pursuant to the Illinois FOIA, I am requesting copies of all annual TIF district reports filed with the State Comptroller for the {STREET} TIF District for fiscal years {DATE} through {DATE}. I also request any developer agreements executed within the district during that period. Please advise on fees, if any. I can accept electronic copies via email. This request is submitted on behalf of {BIZ}.

subject_tmpl: IDOT Mannheim Road Corridor Study — Village Input Requested
body_tmpl: Karen, I'm {FIRST} {LAST} from IDOT's District 1 planning office. We're launching a corridor access management study for Mannheim Road between North Avenue and St. Charles Road, covering multiple municipalities. We'd like Bellwood's input on access points, future development plans, and any pending driveway permits along your segment. Could your office provide a point of contact and any GIS or planning data you're willing to share? The study kickoff is {DATE}.
reply_tmpl: Thank you {FIRST}. Tom Reyes, our village engineer, will be Bellwood's primary contact for this study. I'm copying him here. Tom can share our capital improvement plans and any pending access permits along our Mannheim segment. Please loop him into all technical working group meetings going forward.

subject_tmpl: Change of Use Permit — From Retail to Fitness Studio, {ADDRESS} {STREET}
body_tmpl: I am planning to convert the former retail space at {ADDRESS} {STREET} into a fitness studio under the name {BIZ}. I understand a change-of-use permit is required. The space is approximately {NUM} square feet. I anticipate up to {NUM} clients at peak capacity. What triggers special occupancy review and will we need additional egress, sprinkler upgrades, or ADA work? I'd prefer to know the full scope before finalizing my buildout budget.
reply_tmpl: Good question, {FIRST}. A change of use to assembly/fitness does trigger a full occupancy review. At {NUM} occupants you'll likely need a sprinkler system if one isn't already in place, and ADA-compliant restrooms and accessible entry. I recommend scheduling a pre-application meeting — call our office at {PHONE} to set it up. Catching issues early will save you time and money.

subject_tmpl: Please Recruit More Restaurants to {STREET}
body_tmpl: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, I'm {FIRST} {LAST} and I've lived near {STREET} and {CROSS} for {AGE} years. Our neighborhood badly needs sit-down dining options. Right now we have to drive to Maywood or Hillside for anything besides fast food. I know the Village can't force businesses to come here, but are there grants or tax breaks you could advertise to attract a good family restaurant? I'd be happy to help spread the word to our neighborhood association if you have any materials.

subject_tmpl: TIF Revenue Projection Update — {STREET} District, FY{DATE}
body_tmpl: Karen, I've completed the midyear TIF revenue review for the {STREET} district. Incremental EAV is running about {NUM} percent ahead of last year's projection, driven by the {BIZ} buildout that went on the tax rolls in {DATE}. Projected increment revenue for the full fiscal year is {AMOUNT}. Given the healthy balance, this would be a good cycle to discuss eligible project expenditures — particularly the {STREET} streetscape Phase 2 design. Recommend we add this to the next admin team agenda.

subject_tmpl: Outdoor Seating / Sidewalk Cafe Permit — {BIZ}
body_tmpl: Hello, I'm {FIRST} {LAST}, manager of {BIZ} at {ADDRESS} {STREET}. We'd like to add six sidewalk tables starting {DATE}. The sidewalk in front of our space is about {NUM} feet wide and we plan to leave a clear walking path of at least five feet. Do we need a Village encroachment permit, a separate liquor endorsement since we serve beer and wine, and any insurance certificate? Just want to make sure we have everything before we put the tables out.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, yes — outdoor seating on the public sidewalk requires a Sidewalk Encroachment Permit from our office plus a $1 million liability insurance certificate naming the Village as additional insured. If you'll serve alcohol outdoors you'll also need a Liquor Commission endorsement; contact the Village Clerk's office for that piece. Once I have your site sketch and insurance cert I can usually turn the encroachment permit around in {DAYS} business days.

subject_tmpl: Thank You — Ribbon Cutting for {BIZ} on {DATE}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, thank you and your team for attending our ribbon cutting on {DATE}. Having the Mayor present meant a great deal to us and to our customers who turned out that morning. The coverage in the local paper was wonderful and we've already seen a noticeable uptick in walk-in traffic. We're proud to call Bellwood home and look forward to being part of this community for many years. Please let us know if there is ever anything {BIZ} can do to support Village events.

subject_tmpl: Infrastructure Capacity Check — Developer RFI, {STREET} and {CROSS}
body_tmpl: Tom, I have a developer inquiry for a potential mixed-use project at {ADDRESS} {STREET} — approximately {NUM} residential units plus {NUM} sq ft of retail. Before I respond I need a quick feasibility check: can the existing water main on {STREET} handle the additional demand, and is the sanitary sewer on {CROSS} adequate for the projected flows? Also flag if there are any known stormwater concerns on that block. Please give me a preliminary read by {DATE}.
reply_tmpl: Karen, preliminary review shows the {STREET} water main is a 6-inch cast iron line — that's tight for a project of this scale and a main replacement or upsizing should be a condition of development. Sanitary capacity looks okay based on current flows. There is a known detention issue on the {CROSS} side; developer will need to provide on-site retention. I can put this in writing for the developer meeting if needed.

subject_tmpl: Concern About Construction Hours at New Development, {STREET}
body_tmpl: Good morning, I'm {FIRST} {LAST} and I live on {STREET} near {CROSS}. The construction crew at the new {BIZ} project starts work before 7 a.m. — sometimes as early as {TIME}. The noise wakes my family up and our baby has been particularly affected. Does Bellwood have rules about when construction can start? Is there a number I should call when it happens again?
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, Bellwood's noise ordinance restricts construction activity to 7:00 a.m.–9:00 p.m. on weekdays. If work begins before 7 a.m., please call our non-emergency line at {PHONE} and request a Code Enforcement response. I'm also noting your complaint and will contact the general contractor directly about the permitted hours. Thank you for bringing this to our attention.

subject_tmpl: Enterprise Zone Benefits — Does Bellwood Participate?
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} from {BIZ}. We are exploring sites for a small manufacturing expansion and our accountant asked whether Bellwood falls within an Illinois Enterprise Zone, which would affect our eligibility for state tax credits on equipment and building materials. Could someone in the Village confirm our status and, if applicable, provide the zone administrator's contact information? The property we're looking at is near {STREET} and {CROSS}.

subject_tmpl: Liquor License Application — {BIZ}, {ADDRESS} {STREET}
body_tmpl: Karen, we received a complete liquor license application from {BIZ} at {ADDRESS} {STREET} on {DATE}. The applicant is seeking a Class B on-premise license. Background check and fingerprints have been submitted to the State. Local business license is current (no violations). The required {DAYS}-day public notice period runs through {DATE}. I'll schedule the Liquor Commission hearing for {DATE} unless I hear otherwise. Please let me know if the Mayor has any flags on this one.
reply_tmpl: Thanks Yolanda. No flags from this office. Please proceed with the hearing schedule. Send me a copy of the application summary ahead of the {DATE} meeting.

subject_tmpl: Demolition Permit Inquiry — {ADDRESS} {STREET}
body_tmpl: We are the contract purchaser for {ADDRESS} {STREET} and plan to demolish the existing one-story commercial structure to make way for a new {BIZ} facility. What does Bellwood require for a demolition permit — asbestos survey, utility disconnection letters, bond? Also, is there a minimum notice period before we can begin? The purchase closes on {DATE} and we'd like to break ground within {DAYS} days after closing if possible.
reply_tmpl: Demolition at that address will require: (1) a licensed asbestos/environmental inspector's clearance letter, (2) written utility disconnection confirmations from ComEd, Nicor, and Comcast, (3) a $5,000 demolition bond payable to the Village, and (4) a dust and debris management plan. Once we have those items, permit issuance typically takes {DAYS} business days. Call our office at {PHONE} to schedule a pre-demo meeting.

subject_tmpl: West Cook County Chamber — Business Retention Survey Results
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, the West Cook County Chamber has completed its annual business retention survey and I wanted to share Bellwood-specific highlights before the public release on {DATE}. Overall satisfaction among your corridor businesses is {NUM} percent — up from last year. Top concerns were parking availability and permit processing speed. I'd be glad to present the full results to your staff team if that would be useful. Let me know and I'll have something scheduled before the Chamber's {DATE} board meeting.

subject_tmpl: Property Tax Incentive for Renovation — {ADDRESS} {STREET}
body_tmpl: I am the owner of the commercial building at {ADDRESS} {STREET} and I'm planning a significant renovation — new roof, HVAC replacement, and full interior gut — estimated at {AMOUNT}. A colleague told me Cook County has a Class 6b commercial incentive that can reduce our assessed value for a period of years. Does the Village need to provide a supporting resolution or letter? And is there a deadline tied to when construction begins?
reply_tmpl: Hello, yes — the Cook County Class 6b incentive does require a Village resolution of support before you can apply to the County Assessor. The Village Board typically acts on these at a regular meeting with {DAYS} days' notice. Please submit a written request to the Village Manager's office with your project scope, timeline, and investment amount and we will schedule it for the next available meeting. Construction should not begin until after the County approves the application.

subject_tmpl: Development Agreement Update — {BIZ} Project, {STREET}
body_tmpl: Mayor, wanted to give you a quick heads up before Tuesday's meeting. The {BIZ} development team has signed the letter of intent and our attorney has circulated the first draft of the development agreement. Key open items are the TIF reimbursement schedule, the affordable unit covenant, and the public parking easement on the north end of the site. The developer wants to close these points by {DATE} to keep their construction financing timeline intact. I recommend we schedule a closed session with legal and the developer's counsel the week of {DATE}.

subject_tmpl: Drive-Through Addition — Zoning and Site Plan Questions, {STREET}
body_tmpl: I operate {BIZ} at {ADDRESS} {STREET} and am exploring adding a drive-through lane to our existing building. I know this is a significant site plan change. My main questions: Is a drive-through a permitted use in our current B-2 zoning, or does it require a special use permit? What stacking distance requirements apply? And will we need a full traffic study? Happy to meet in person if that's easier.

subject_tmpl: Concerns About Proposed {BIZ} on {STREET}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I'm {FIRST} {LAST}, a homeowner on {STREET} near {CROSS}. I've heard a {BIZ} is being proposed for the corner lot at {ADDRESS}. I have serious concerns about increased traffic, late-night noise, and the type of clientele such a business would attract to our block. I'd like to know when this is going before the Village Board and how residents can formally weigh in. My neighbors and I plan to attend whatever public hearing is scheduled.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for writing, {FIRST}. Any special use or zoning variance for that address would require a public hearing before the Zoning Board of Appeals with posted notice at least {DAYS} days in advance. I've asked my office to flag you when the application is formally submitted. Your voice and your neighbors' voices matter in this process and I encourage you to attend and testify.

subject_tmpl: Grant Opportunity — CMAP Local Technical Assistance, Corridor Planning
body_tmpl: Karen, CMAP is accepting applications for Local Technical Assistance grants — free planning services for corridor studies and small area plans. The {STREET} corridor is a strong candidate given our documented vacancy rate and the pending developer interest near {CROSS}. The application deadline is {DATE} and requires a Board resolution of support. If you want to pursue this, I'd need authorization to draft the application this week. CMAP studies typically run {DAYS} months and could include market analysis, streetscape concepts, and zoning recommendations.
reply_tmpl: Tom, yes — please draft the application. I'll get a resolution on the {DATE} Board agenda. Loop in the Mayor's office so we can prepare a brief summary statement from the Mayor to attach.

subject_tmpl: Abandoned Property Becoming a Blight — {ADDRESS} {STREET}
body_tmpl: Mayor, I'm writing about the former commercial building at {ADDRESS} {STREET} that has been sitting abandoned for over {DAYS} days. The roof is visibly sagging, graffiti covers the side wall, and there's standing water in the parking lot every time it rains. This property is dragging down property values on our block and making the whole area feel unsafe. What enforcement tools does the Village have to compel the owner to rehabilitate or sell? Please advise.
reply_tmpl: Thank you {FIRST}. I've asked our Building Official and Code Enforcement to inspect {ADDRESS} {STREET} this week and document all violations. If the structure is found to be dangerous or blighted, we have authority under the Illinois Property Maintenance Code to issue orders and, ultimately, to proceed with municipal remediation and lien. I understand your frustration and we will keep the pressure on.

subject_tmpl: Shared Parking Agreement Request — {BIZ} and Village Lot on {STREET}
body_tmpl: I'm {FIRST} {LAST}, owner of {BIZ} at {ADDRESS} {STREET}. We're short {NUM} parking spaces for our planned expansion and noticed the Village-owned lot on {STREET} near {CROSS} appears to have available capacity in the evenings and weekends when we need it most. Is the Village open to a shared parking or lease agreement for those {NUM} spaces? Our peak need is {TIME} to {TIME} Thursday through Sunday. Happy to discuss terms.

subject_tmpl: Home Occupation Permit — Can I Run {BIZ} from My House?
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} here — I live at {ADDRESS} {STREET} and recently started a small graphic design business called {BIZ}. I work entirely from home with no clients visiting and no employees. I see the Village requires a home occupation permit. Can you tell me what documentation I need and whether there are any restrictions I should know about — signage, deliveries, that sort of thing? I want to make sure I'm operating legally.
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, a home occupation permit for a no-client, no-employee business like yours is fairly straightforward. You'll need to complete our Home Occupation Permit application (available on the Village website), pay the {AMOUNT} fee, and agree to the standard conditions — no exterior signage, no business-related vehicle parking on the street, and no receipt of commercial deliveries that generate neighborhood nuisance. Processing usually takes {DAYS} business days.

subject_tmpl: Scheduling Heads Up — Developer Site Walk, {DATE}
body_tmpl: Karen, the Mayor has confirmed he can join the {BIZ} development team for a site walk at {ADDRESS} {STREET} on {DATE} at {TIME}. Please let Frank DiMeo and Tom Reyes know so they can attend if their schedules allow — the developer will likely have infrastructure questions. The Mayor's hard out is {TIME}. I'll send a calendar hold to all parties once you confirm the group.
